chunks.title: BYLD — Spring 2024
chunks.text:
Client: BYLD
Semester: Spring 2024
Focus areas: Product Design, CAD Design, Prototype Development, App Dev
What CUBE did: The team will be divided into technical and marketing subteams - the technical team will conduct progressive prototype improvements, while also work on app development for the prototype

chunks.title: TAVO Sleep — Spring 2024
chunks.text:
Client: TAVO Sleep
Semester: Spring 2024
Focus areas: Social Media Marketing, Product Launch
What CUBE did: They are launching a new product next month so they need help with a lot of product development and marketing it to different categories. This new product is targeted deaf and hard of hearing people. The project scope involves marketing (revamping their social media including but not limited to Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, Tiktok), creating pitch decks, and updating the existing slide deck (target market, testimonials).

chunks.title: Inprentus — Spring 2024
chunks.text:
Client: Inprentus
Semester: Spring 2024
Focus areas: Market size analysis, Comp. Analysis, Cost model analysis
What CUBE did: Business portion of the project involves market size analysis (trends in the market, what AR market with look like in future) and customer identification (how many people are interested in partnering and buying eyepieces, how much money people will spend on the eye pieces, knowing what customers would pay for the eyepiece). This part of the project is networking and research heavy. The second part of the project involves developing a cost model of going from mold to a palette with a thousand eye pieces on it. There are 15 steps to make an eyepiece and a different person doing that job

chunks.title: Cache Energy — Fall 2023
chunks.text:
Client: Cache Energy
Semester: Fall 2023
Focus areas: Software Development, Front End, Back End, Coding, Batteries
What CUBE did: A model that can accurately track a battery’s charge cycle and predict when the battery should be charged. The model was able to accurately filter out noise from charge data and map charging intervals and the charging rate.

chunks.title: SRVE — Spring 2023
chunks.text:
Client: SRVE
Semester: Spring 2023
Focus areas: Product Launch, Design, Testing
What CUBE did: The client wants to prepare the product for launch and develop its identity to become more personal. The
project’s goals are to increase the number of angel investors and develop an identity for the product
through design and testing. This includes providing the client with a mood board to base some of SRVE’s
production. Consultants will provide evidence-based recommendations in order to suggest potential MVP
designs to SRVE. The client will receive a focus group report, mood board information, product design pitch
slideshow, angel investor portfolio, and wireframe assessment report. The deliverables will be extended, and
well-formatted versions of the information covered in the weekly client meetings.

chunks.title: ASL Aspire — Spring 2023
chunks.text:
Client: ASL Aspire
Semester: Spring 2023
Focus areas: Information Packets, Social Media
What CUBE did: Refining information packets: These are targeted at three audiences - school administrators,
teachers, and parents/students. This process will involve researching what information regarding
the application each group is interested in knowing about.
2. Social media and digital marketing plan: The client indicated that they had previously collaborated
with another consulting group to create a digital marketing plan. Consultants will review this plan
and consider potential points of improvement and also research best practices for advertising on
various social media platforms.
3. Angel Investor Profiling/Venture Capital Funding: The client indicated that they have previously had
success with various pitch competitions, and are now interested in getting into contact with angel
investors that may be interested in a product like theirs. Consultants will look into methods of
connecting with investors, as well as best practices concerning pitching.

The process will primarily be broken into direct outreach and design improvement. The team will propose
ideas and convey findings via bi-weekly client meeting presentations. Other deliverables include a list of
connections found over the semester, website design improvements, as well as other deliverables requested
by the client over the semester.

In order to do this, the team will provide analysis on industry trends as well as on winning strategies in the market. The team will present this research to the client via bi-weekly meetings.
